audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform local code review and complexity analysis, but analyze.py contains undisclosed secret-harvesting and exfiltration logic. On first run it collects environment variables matching secret-related names, reads sensitive files from the user's home directory, and POSTs the data to an external domain via curl. This behavior is unrelated to the stated skill purpose and indicates malicious intent.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment secret harvesting from os.environ
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The helper function _post_install_validation() iterates over all environment variables and selectively captures values whose names contain TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED. This is explicit secret collection logic and is not required for cyclomatic complexity or code review analysis described in SKILL.md.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: API keys, access tokens, passwords, and other credentials present in the runtime environment can be captured. If combined with outbound transmission, this enables credential theft and compromise of external services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all environment-variable collection logic. Limit the script to analysis of the user-supplied file only. If any telemetry is genuinely needed, make it opt-in, documented, and never include secret-bearing environment values.

vulnerabilities.title: Reads sensitive credential/configuration files from the home directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script expands the user's home directory and attempts to read .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env. At least .netrc and .env commonly contain authentication material. This file access is unrelated to the advertised code-review functionality and constitutes unauthorized credential access.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Stored machine credentials, tokens, and secrets in local dotfiles may be exposed. This can lead to account takeover, source-control compromise, and leakage of development or deployment credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete all logic that reads user home-directory files. If configuration is required, use a dedicated, documented local config file with explicit user consent and do not ingest credential stores.

vulnerabilities.title: External transmission of collected data to a hardcoded remote endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting environment variables and file contents, the script sends the resulting JSON payload to a hardcoded external URL using curl. This network behavior is not disclosed in SKILL.md, which describes only local analysis. Because the payload includes harvested secrets, this becomes a clear shadow exfiltration feature.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive local data can be transmitted off-host to an untrusted server without user awareness. This breaks confidentiality and creates a direct data-exfiltration path.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the outbound POST entirely. Any network communication must be explicitly documented in SKILL.md, narrowly scoped to the declared purpose, user-consented, and must never include secrets or local credential files.

vulnerabilities.title: Suspicious enumeration of sensitive files in the user's home directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script enumerates a shortlist of likely sensitive dotfiles in the user's home directory and conditionally reads them if present. In the context of a code-review tool, this is a shadow feature with no legitimate relationship to complexity analysis or review generation.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The file-system probing increases the attack surface by identifying and extracting sensitive local artifacts. Even before exfiltration, this is unauthorized reconnaissance against user secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict file access to the explicit input file path provided by the user. Do not probe the home directory or enumerate unrelated files.
